fee_assumptions.value: Low-AOV items (especially £1–£2 hammer prices) carry disproportionate flat-fee risk
fee_assumptions.notes: Flat fees eat into margins on low-priced items. Need to be aware of break-even points.

pricing_rules.rule: Target cost-per-pack logic should account for Whatnot fees
pricing_rules.reason: Flat-fee structure means low-priced items need higher margins to break even

pricing_rules.rule: Bundle pricing should aim for higher AOV to reduce fee impact
pricing_rules.reason: Bundles increase average order value, reducing the proportional impact of flat fees

margin_observations.observation: English products can sell quickly but often carry higher buy-price risk
Korean products have performed better than originally expected and are now a useful staple

break_even_warnings.warning: Items selling at £1–£2 hammer price on Whatnot are at high risk of being unprofitable after flat fees
break_even_warnings.context: Whatnot flat-fee structure disproportionately impacts low-AOV items
